title: Gauge and Instrument Display Research Catalog
scope_note: Mechanism-first research catalogue; generic framebuffer/HDMI displays excluded.

date_confidence_legend.H: Dated primary, official or museum support
date_confidence_legend.M: Approximate adoption/common-use range from reliable overviews

entries.name: Liquid-column manometer: U-tube, well-type and inclined
entries.representative_names_models: Open/closed U-tubes; cistern/well manometers; inclined draft gauges
entries.measured_or_indicated: Gauge, differential or absolute pressure
entries.era: 1640s-present; inclined industrial forms common from late 1800s
quirks_to_simulate: Moving meniscus, capillary depression and wetting
inclined scales magnify low pressure and become strongly nonlinear if geometry is wrong

image_links.title: Wikimedia Commons images: U-tube inclined manometer

entries.name: Mercury barometer: cistern, Fortin and wheel forms
entries.representative_names_models: Torricellian barometer; Fortin adjustable cistern; wheel barometer with float and pulley
entries.measured_or_indicated: Atmospheric pressure
entries.era: 1643-present; mercury versions now restricted but still historical/reference instruments
quirks_to_simulate: wheel mechanisms add backlash and pointer lag

image_links.title: Wikimedia Commons images: Fortin wheel mercury barometer

entries.name: Aneroid barometer and pressure capsule mechanism
entries.representative_names_models: Lucien Vidi-style evacuated capsule; wall barometers; aircraft altimeter capsules
entries.measured_or_indicated: Atmospheric pressure or altitude via pressure
quirks_to_simulate: pointer may respond to a tap
altitude versions wrap repeatedly and can be misread by a full revolution

sources.title: FAA: Pilot’s Handbook of Aeronautical Knowledge, Flight Instruments

image_links.title: Wikimedia Commons images: aneroid barometer mechanism

entries.name: Bourdon-tube pressure gauge, including C, spiral and helical tubes
entries.representative_names_models: Eugène Bourdon; WIKA 213.53; Ashcroft Duragauge; Budenberg locomotive gauges
entries.measured_or_indicated: Gauge, absolute, vacuum or compound pressure

sources.title: Science Museum: Musgrave Bourdon gauge object record

image_links.title: Wikimedia Commons images: Bourdon tube pressure gauge

entries.name: Diaphragm pressure gauge
entries.representative_names_models: Corrugated diaphragm gauges; chemical seals; sanitary diaphragm gauges
entries.measured_or_indicated: Low pressure, differential pressure, viscous or corrosive process pressure
quirks_to_simulate: Deflection is nonlinear without linkage compensation
diaphragm can snap or oil-can under abuse
broad, shallow cases and large pointer travel

image_links.title: Wikimedia Commons images: diaphragm pressure gauge

entries.name: Capsule and bellows pressure gauges
entries.representative_names_models: Stacked evacuated capsules; aneroid capsules; metallic bellows transmitters
entries.measured_or_indicated: Very low gas pressure, differential pressure, altitude or displacement
quirks_to_simulate: bellows can breathe, creep or buckle
capsule stacks may have slight stepwise motion

image_links.title: Wikimedia Commons images: capsule bellows pressure gauge

entries.name: Magnetic-coupled differential gauge
entries.representative_names_models: Dwyer Magnehelic, introduced 1953
entries.measured_or_indicated: Low differential pressure, filter loading, clean-room pressure and air velocity
quirks_to_simulate: Diaphragm motion couples through magnet and helix with little gear friction
pointer can oscillate with fan pulses
overpressure relief and a characteristic broad 270-degree scale

sources.title: DwyerOmega: Making of the Magnehelic gauge

image_links.title: Wikimedia Commons images: Dwyer Magnehelic gauge

entries.name: Compound, duplex and twin-pointer pressure gauges
entries.representative_names_models: Vacuum-plus-pressure compound scale; locomotive brake duplex gauges; dual-process pointers
entries.measured_or_indicated: Two pressures, pressure plus vacuum, or one value over a signed range
quirks_to_simulate: Two pointers may cross or obscure each other
compound scales often compress one side
operator can mistake which needle belongs to which circuit

image_links.title: Wikimedia Commons images: duplex compound pressure gauge

entries.name: Liquid-filled, snubbed and damped pressure gauge
entries.representative_names_models: Glycerine-, silicone- or halocarbon-filled cases; needle valves and pulsation snubbers
entries.measured_or_indicated: Pressure in vibrating or pulsating machinery

image_links.title: Wikimedia Commons images: glycerin filled pressure gauge bubble

entries.name: Contact, alarm and meter-relay gauge
entries.representative_names_models: Adjustable red/green set pointers; snap contacts; inductive contacts; pressure and temperature switch gauges
entries.measured_or_indicated: Measured value plus alarm or control threshold
quirks_to_simulate: Movable set bugs sit above or below the pointer
contact drag causes a tiny hitch
snap action and chatter near threshold

sources.title: WIKA switch contacts in measuring instruments

image_links.title: Wikimedia Commons images: contact pressure gauge adjustable alarm pointers

entries.name: Drag, tell-tale and witness-pointer gauge
entries.representative_names_models: Peak-hold red hand; min/max memory pointers; resettable maximum pressure and tachometer indicators
entries.measured_or_indicated: Maximum, minimum or both extrema of pressure, speed, temperature or load
quirks_to_simulate: Main hand physically pushes a loose memory hand
manual reset can disturb the main pointer
witness may creep, stick or be left at an old event

image_links.title: Wikimedia Commons images: maximum drag pointer pressure gauge

entries.name: McLeod compression vacuum gauge
entries.representative_names_models: Herbert McLeod gauge; laboratory mercury compression gauges
entries.measured_or_indicated: Low absolute pressure by compressing a known gas volume
entries.era: 1874-c.1970s common; still used as a reference in some laboratories
quirks_to_simulate: Reading requires trapping and compressing gas

image_links.title: Wikimedia Commons images: McLeod vacuum gauge

entries.name: Thermal-conductivity vacuum gauges: Pirani and thermocouple
entries.representative_names_models: Marcello Pirani gauge; convection-enhanced Pirani; thermocouple vacuum gauge
entries.measured_or_indicated: Rough and medium vacuum inferred from heat loss
quirks_to_simulate: poor response near atmosphere and high vacuum
zero and span drift with contamination

sources.title: Leybold: Marcello Pirani and the Pirani gauge

image_links.title: Wikimedia Commons images: Pirani thermocouple vacuum gauge

entries.name: Cold-cathode ionisation gauge
entries.representative_names_models: Penning gauge; inverted magnetron gauge
entries.measured_or_indicated: High vacuum
quirks_to_simulate: Needs ignition and may suddenly jump on
hysteresis between striking and sustaining pressure
arc-like flashes and dropouts are authentic failure modes

sources.title: CERN: Gauges for fine and high vacuum

image_links.title: Wikimedia Commons images: Penning cold cathode vacuum gauge

entries.name: Hot-cathode ionisation gauge
entries.representative_names_models: Bayard-Alpert gauge; triode ion gauge
entries.measured_or_indicated: High and ultra-high vacuum
quirks_to_simulate: abrupt overpressure trip to protect filament
readings may span many decades on logarithmic scales

sources.title: CERN: Gauges for fine and high vacuum

image_links.title: Wikimedia Commons images: Bayard Alpert ionization gauge

entries.name: Capacitance diaphragm manometer
entries.representative_names_models: MKS Baratron and similar heated capacitance manometers
entries.measured_or_indicated: Absolute or differential pressure, especially process vacuum
quirks_to_simulate: True force measurement is largely gas independent
excellent linearity but limited range per sensor
digital controllers often add range switching

image_links.title: Wikimedia Commons images: MKS Baratron capacitance manometer display

entries.name: Tubular, reflex and transparent sight-level gauges
entries.representative_names_models: Boiler gauge glass; reflex prism glass; transparent double-plate level gauge
entries.measured_or_indicated: Liquid level, phase boundary or boiler water level
quirks_to_simulate: staining and scale obscure the line
refraction makes liquid dark/light in reflex glass
drain and blow-down events make the level leap

image_links.title: Wikimedia Commons images: boiler reflex sight glass level gauge

entries.name: Float-and-tape tank gauge
entries.representative_names_models: Mechanical tank gauging tape; Whessoe and Varec-style systems
entries.measured_or_indicated: Large storage-tank liquid level
quirks_to_simulate: Counterweight motion, cable sag and pulley friction
scale can run vertically or drive a dial

image_links.title: Wikimedia Commons images: float tape tank level gauge

entries.name: Magnetic level indicator with flip flags
entries.representative_names_models: External chamber, magnetic float and red/white or black/yellow rotating flags
entries.measured_or_indicated: Liquid level or interface level
quirks_to_simulate: Flags flip discretely in a travelling wave
one flag can stick or reverse
level is quantised by flag pitch
interface floats can produce two boundaries

image_links.title: Wikimedia Commons images: magnetic level indicator flip flags

entries.name: Variable-area flowmeter / rotameter
entries.representative_names_models: KROHNE Rotameter; Brooks Sho-Rate; glass or armoured metal tubes
entries.measured_or_indicated: Volumetric or mass flow after calibration
quirks_to_simulate: Float bobs, spins and may chatter under pulsation
read at a specified edge of the float
density, viscosity, pressure and temperature affect calibration

sources.title: Brooks Instrument: Rotameter history and operating principle

image_links.title: Wikimedia Commons images: glass tube rotameter float

entries.name: Liquid-in-glass thermometer
entries.representative_names_models: Mercury, alcohol, kerosene and spirit thermometers; clinical and industrial stem types
entries.measured_or_indicated: Temperature
quirks_to_simulate: alcohol can wet the glass and produce a fuzzy edge

image_links.title: Wikimedia Commons images: liquid in glass thermometer mercury alcohol

entries.name: Six’s maximum-minimum thermometer
entries.representative_names_models: James Six design; U-tube with two markers and magnetic reset
entries.measured_or_indicated: Maximum and minimum temperature since reset
quirks_to_simulate: alcohol/mercury interfaces move separate index markers
markers stay put through surface tension
markers can stick, tilt or lag
users often read the wrong end

image_links.title: Wikimedia Commons images: Six maximum minimum thermometer

entries.name: Bimetal dial thermometer
entries.representative_names_models: Spiral, helical and cantilever bimetal instruments; WIKA and Ashcroft industrial dials
entries.measured_or_indicated: Temperature
quirks_to_simulate: pointer may creep after temperature stabilises
long helical coils give broad sweep

image_links.title: Wikimedia Commons images: bimetal dial thermometer

entries.name: Filled-system capillary thermometer
entries.representative_names_models: Gas-, liquid- or vapour-actuated bulb, capillary and Bourdon receiver
entries.measured_or_indicated: Remote temperature
quirks_to_simulate: bubbles or leaks cause permanent offset
pointer can move after the process has already stopped changing

image_links.title: Wikimedia Commons images: capillary gas actuated thermometer dial

entries.name: Thermochromic liquid-crystal strip and leuco-dye indicator
entries.representative_names_models: Forehead strips; aquarium strips; battery/test labels; reversible and irreversible temperature labels
entries.measured_or_indicated: Surface temperature or threshold exposure
quirks_to_simulate: irreversible labels retain an event and cannot be smoothly animated

image_links.title: Wikimedia Commons images: thermochromic liquid crystal temperature strip

entries.name: Hair hygrometer and hygrograph
entries.representative_names_models: Human- or horse-hair bundles; Saussure-type hygrometer; clockwork hygrograph
entries.measured_or_indicated: Relative humidity
quirks_to_simulate: Hair length response is nonlinear and has hysteresis
linkage is shaped to linearise the scale
chart versions add pen friction, clock drift and a daily/weekly trace

sources.title: Met Office Library: Hair hygrometer response

image_links.title: Wikimedia Commons images: hair hygrometer hygrograph

entries.name: Wet- and dry-bulb psychrometer
entries.representative_names_models: Sling psychrometer; Assmann aspirated psychrometer; Stevenson-screen pair
entries.measured_or_indicated: Humidity, dew point or wet-bulb temperature
quirks_to_simulate: Requires a genuinely wet clean wick and airflow
two separate columns can have different lag and calibration

sources.title: Met Office: Wet- and dry-bulb thermometer observations

image_links.title: Wikimedia Commons images: sling Assmann psychrometer wet dry bulb

entries.name: Hydrometer and floating density gauges
entries.representative_names_models: Lactometer, alcoholmeter, API hydrometer, battery hydrometer
entries.measured_or_indicated: Liquid density or concentration
quirks_to_simulate: read top or bottom of meniscus depending standard
bubbles stuck to the stem make it read light

image_links.title: Wikimedia Commons images: glass hydrometer floating scale

entries.name: Galileo thermometer
entries.representative_names_models: Sealed column with weighted glass bulbs and temperature tags
entries.measured_or_indicated: Approximate ambient temperature
quirks_to_simulate: bulbs hover, cluster and swap order slowly
two adjacent bulbs can straddle the answer
visually rich but not precision instrumentation

image_links.title: Wikimedia Commons images: Galileo thermometer floating bulbs

entries.name: Rain gauge and tipping-bucket recorder
entries.representative_names_models: Standard cylindrical gauge; siphon gauge; tipping bucket with reed switch
entries.measured_or_indicated: Rainfall depth and rate
quirks_to_simulate: mechanical bounce may generate duplicate electrical pulses

image_links.title: Wikimedia Commons images: tipping bucket rain gauge mechanism

entries.name: Cup, propeller and vane anemometers
entries.representative_names_models: Robinson cup anemometer; cup-and-vane station instruments; mechanical dial windmeters
entries.measured_or_indicated: Wind speed and direction
quirks_to_simulate: mechanical dials can flutter while totalisers ratchet

image_links.title: Wikimedia Commons images: mechanical cup anemometer dial

entries.name: Bubble level, ball inclinometer and pendulum clinometer
entries.representative_names_models: Spirit level; aircraft slip-skid ball; marine clinometer; pendulum angle gauge
entries.measured_or_indicated: Tilt, bank, slope or lateral acceleration
quirks_to_simulate: Bubble/ball has viscous damping and wall friction
curved tube creates nonlinear angle mapping
temperature changes fluid viscosity and bubble size
aircraft ball responds to combined gravity and lateral acceleration, not bank angle alone

image_links.title: Wikimedia Commons images: ball inclinometer slip skid bubble

entries.name: Liquid magnetic compass card
entries.representative_names_models: Marine bowl compass; aircraft whiskey compass; floating card with lubber line
entries.measured_or_indicated: Magnetic heading
quirks_to_simulate: card can lag then swing past
glass and fluid add lens distortion and parallax

sources.title: FAA: Pilot’s Handbook of Aeronautical Knowledge, Flight Instruments

image_links.title: Wikimedia Commons images: liquid magnetic compass card aircraft marine

entries.name: Diving depth gauge: capillary and Bourdon
entries.representative_names_models: Open-ended capillary gauge; Bourdon depth gauge; combined analog depth/pressure consoles
entries.measured_or_indicated: Water depth
quirks_to_simulate: Capillary scale is severely compressed with depth by Boyle’s law
Bourdon versions show hysteresis and drag-pointer maxima
both can retain bubbles behind the lens

image_links.title: Wikimedia Commons images: vintage capillary diving depth gauge

entries.name: Mercury and aneroid sphygmomanometer
entries.representative_names_models: Riva-Rocci mercury column; mechanical aneroid dial; hospital wall models
entries.measured_or_indicated: Cuff pressure and blood pressure during auscultation
quirks_to_simulate: aneroid gear hysteresis and shock damage
operator timing and Korotkoff sounds are part of the reading ritual

image_links.title: Wikimedia Commons images: mercury aneroid sphygmomanometer gauge

entries.name: Quartz-fibre self-reading pocket dosimeter
entries.representative_names_models: Lauritsen electroscope; Victoreen Model 541; Landsverk pocket dosimeters
entries.measured_or_indicated: Accumulated ionising-radiation dose
entries.era: 1937-c.1990s common; collectors and limited specialist use today
quirks_to_simulate: Tiny hairline viewed through an eyepiece
reading depends on illumination and eye alignment

sources.title: ORAU: Measuring radiation exposure then and now

image_links.title: Wikimedia Commons images: quartz fiber pocket dosimeter Victoreen

entries.name: Spring balance, dial force gauge and dynamometer
entries.representative_names_models: Salter spring balance; Chatillon dial force gauge; crane dynamometer
entries.measured_or_indicated: Force, weight or tension
quirks_to_simulate: nonlinear scale in some lever mechanisms

image_links.title: Wikimedia Commons images: vintage dial force gauge spring balance

entries.name: Dial indicator, test indicator and dial torque wrench
entries.representative_names_models: Starrett/Mitutoyo plunger indicator; lever test indicator; beam/dial torque wrench
entries.measured_or_indicated: Small displacement, runout or applied torque
quirks_to_simulate: pointer can whirl through multiple turns
torque versions use a drag maximum hand and elastic-beam hysteresis

image_links.title: Wikimedia Commons images: dial indicator torque wrench drag pointer

entries.name: Mechanical tachometer and eddy-current speedometer
entries.representative_names_models: Centrifugal tachometers; magnetic drag-cup speedometers; Smiths and Jaeger instruments
entries.measured_or_indicated: Rotational speed or road speed
quirks_to_simulate: eddy-current drag depends on magnet strength and temperature

sources.title: Smiths Instruments: History of the tachometer

image_links.title: Wikimedia Commons images: vintage Smiths eddy current speedometer tachometer

entries.name: Chronometric tachometer
entries.representative_names_models: Smiths chronometric racing and aircraft tachometers; escapement-and-clutch mechanism
entries.measured_or_indicated: Rotational speed averaged over timed samples
entries.era: c.1920s-1960s common; still restored and reproduced
quirks_to_simulate: Needle advances in tiny timed steps rather than continuously
lively but oddly steady under vibration

sources.title: Smiths Instruments: History of the tachometer

image_links.title: Wikimedia Commons images: Smiths chronometric tachometer

entries.name: Mechanical odometer, cyclometer and utility-meter register
entries.representative_names_models: Number drums, Geneva carries, pointer sub-dials; gas, water and electricity registers
entries.measured_or_indicated: Distance, volume, energy, cycles or accumulated count
quirks_to_simulate: worn gears may stick then jump

image_links.title: Wikimedia Commons images: mechanical odometer cyclometer utility meter register

entries.name: Strip-chart and circular-chart recorder
entries.representative_names_models: Bristol, Honeywell and Foxboro recorders; clockwork thermographs and barographs
entries.measured_or_indicated: One or more variables over time
quirks_to_simulate: ink blobs, skipping and dried nibs
circular charts create a seam at each revolution

image_links.title: Wikimedia Commons images: vintage strip chart circular chart recorder

entries.name: Stroboscopic tachometer
entries.representative_names_models: Mechanical vibrating-reed strobe; electronic xenon/LED stroboscope
entries.measured_or_indicated: Rotational speed or repeated-motion frequency
quirks_to_simulate: Aliasing is the defining quirk: motion appears stationary at the true rate and at harmonics/subharmonics
flash persistence and ambient light alter readability

image_links.title: Wikimedia Commons images: vintage stroboscopic tachometer

entries.name: Disappearing-filament optical pyrometer
entries.representative_names_models: Holborn-Kurlbaum style; Leeds & Northrup optical pyrometers
entries.measured_or_indicated: High temperature by matching filament brightness
quirks_to_simulate: Operator turns current until filament visually disappears
filament glows from dark red to bright
no contact lag but line-of-sight obstruction and smoke matter

image_links.title: Wikimedia Commons images: disappearing filament optical pyrometer

entries.name: D’Arsonval / Weston moving-coil meter
entries.representative_names_models: Weston Model 60; Simpson 260 movement; panel ammeters and voltmeters
entries.measured_or_indicated: DC current or any transduced quantity
quirks_to_simulate: magnetic shock, bearing friction and pointer balance affect zero

image_links.title: Wikimedia Commons images: Weston moving coil analog meter mirror scale

entries.name: Taut-band meter movement
entries.representative_names_models: Suspended moving coil without pivots; high-grade laboratory and aerospace panel meters
entries.measured_or_indicated: Current, voltage or transduced variables
quirks_to_simulate: Very low friction and no jewel-bearing stiction
delicate bands can twist or break under shock
slower, silkier return than cheap pivots
zero can shift if the case is rotated

image_links.title: Wikimedia Commons images: taut band analog panel meter

entries.name: Moving-iron meter
entries.representative_names_models: Attraction and repulsion types; AC switchboard ammeters and voltmeters
entries.measured_or_indicated: AC or DC current/voltage
quirks_to_simulate: pointer may tremble on distorted AC
residual magnetism leaves a small zero offset

image_links.title: Wikimedia Commons images: moving iron AC panel meter

entries.name: Electrodynamometer instrument
entries.representative_names_models: Siemens electrodynamometer; dynamometer wattmeter
entries.measured_or_indicated: AC/DC current, voltage or true power
quirks_to_simulate: power meters can read signed centre-zero
susceptible to external magnetic fields and lead phase error

image_links.title: Wikimedia Commons images: Siemens electrodynamometer wattmeter

entries.name: Hot-wire ammeter
entries.representative_names_models: Thermal-expansion wire meters in early radio and switchboards
entries.measured_or_indicated: RMS-like AC/RF current
quirks_to_simulate: accidental overload stretches or burns the wire
needle may continue climbing after the signal is removed

image_links.title: Wikimedia Commons images: vintage hot wire ammeter

entries.name: Thermocouple RF meter
entries.representative_names_models: Heater/thermocouple ammeters; vacuum thermocouple RF meters
entries.measured_or_indicated: RF current or power via heating
quirks_to_simulate: tiny signals may not overcome pointer friction

image_links.title: Wikimedia Commons images: thermocouple RF ammeter analog meter

entries.name: Electrostatic voltmeter
entries.representative_names_models: Kelvin attracted-disc; quadrant electrometer; high-voltage panel meters
entries.measured_or_indicated: High voltage or electrostatic potential
quirks_to_simulate: pointer can cling near zero then jump
electrostatic attraction may pull into a stop

image_links.title: Wikimedia Commons images: antique electrostatic voltmeter quadrant electrometer

entries.name: Vibrating-reed frequency meter
entries.representative_names_models: Frahm reed meter; switchboard 45–65 Hz indicators
entries.measured_or_indicated: AC frequency
entries.era: c.1900-present; legacy power panels and generators
quirks_to_simulate: A comb of reeds vibrates, with the resonant one appearing widest or brightest
vibration and viewing angle make the answer ambiguous

image_links.title: Wikimedia Commons images: vibrating reed frequency meter panel

entries.name: Mirror / light-spot galvanometer
entries.representative_names_models: Thomson mirror galvanometer; ballistic galvanometer with projected light spot
entries.measured_or_indicated: Very small current, charge or magnetic-flux change
quirks_to_simulate: No physical pointer: a light spot sweeps a distant scale

image_links.title: Wikimedia Commons images: mirror galvanometer light spot scale

entries.name: String galvanometer
entries.representative_names_models: Einthoven electrocardiograph; photographic string oscillograph
entries.measured_or_indicated: Rapid electrical waveform, especially ECG
quirks_to_simulate: A fine conductive string moves in a magnetic field
overload can snap or stick the string

image_links.title: Wikimedia Commons images: Einthoven string galvanometer ECG

entries.name: Ferraris induction meter
entries.representative_names_models: Rotating-disc electricity meter; Sangamo and Landis & Gyr watt-hour meters
entries.measured_or_indicated: Integrated AC electrical energy
quirks_to_simulate: Disc accelerates and coasts under eddy-current torque
reverse power may run backward unless blocked

image_links.title: Wikimedia Commons images: Ferraris induction watt hour meter rotating disc

entries.name: Cross-coil / ratiometer gauge
entries.representative_names_models: Two-coil fuel and temperature gauges; Wheatstone-bridge ratiometer indicators
entries.measured_or_indicated: Resistance ratio, fuel level, temperature or pressure sender output
quirks_to_simulate: Angle depends on current ratio, reducing supply-voltage sensitivity
open-circuit faults can drive to an extreme

image_links.title: Wikimedia Commons images: cross coil ratiometer fuel gauge movement

entries.name: Thermal bimetal automotive gauge
entries.representative_names_models: King-Seeley and Smiths fuel/temperature gauges; pulsed instrument-voltage regulator systems
entries.measured_or_indicated: Fuel level, coolant temperature, oil pressure by sender resistance
quirks_to_simulate: pulsed regulator can cause a faint periodic quiver
cold start produces a slow crawl from rest

image_links.title: Wikimedia Commons images: vintage bimetal automotive fuel temperature gauge

entries.name: Air-core gauge
entries.representative_names_models: Two perpendicular coils and permanent-magnet rotor; General Motors clusters and industrial indicators
entries.measured_or_indicated: Vehicle speed, fuel, temperature or arbitrary angular command
quirks_to_simulate: No return spring, so power-off position may be arbitrary
rotor can flip to an equivalent angle
supply loss may leave the pointer stranded

image_links.title: Wikimedia Commons images: air core gauge movement instrument cluster

entries.name: Stepper-motor gauge
entries.representative_names_models: Switec/Juken X25 and X27.168; VID29; modern instrument-cluster motors
entries.measured_or_indicated: Digitally commanded speed, RPM, fuel, temperature and other values
quirks_to_simulate: startup homing sweep into a mechanical stop
some motors allow only about 315 degrees, not a full circle

sources.title: Adafruit: Switec/Juken X27.168 gauge stepper specifications

image_links.title: Wikimedia Commons images: Switec X27.168 gauge stepper motor

entries.name: Synchro / selsyn remote indicator
entries.representative_names_models: Bendix and General Electric synchros; ship, aircraft and radar repeaters
entries.measured_or_indicated: Remote angular position such as heading, valve position or antenna bearing
quirks_to_simulate: loss of one phase creates wrong or weak positions
power-off pointer is free or parked

image_links.title: Wikimedia Commons images: synchro selsyn remote position indicator

entries.name: Servo and torque-motor indicator
entries.representative_names_models: Autopilot repeaters; radar bearing indicators; industrial servo meters
entries.measured_or_indicated: Remote position or electrically computed variable
quirks_to_simulate: power loss may drop a warning flag or let the pointer drift
test mode may slew through full scale

image_links.title: Wikimedia Commons images: servo motor analog indicator gauge

entries.name: Magnetic latching flag, ball and shutter annunciator
entries.representative_names_models: Aircraft OFF flags; red/white magnetic balls; relay semaphore indicators
entries.measured_or_indicated: Binary or small-state warning/status indication
quirks_to_simulate: weak coil can leave a half flag
shutter may slide rather than rotate
paint chips and dirty pivots add authenticity

image_links.title: Wikimedia Commons images: aircraft magnetic flag annunciator indicator

entries.name: Contact meter relay / limit meter
entries.representative_names_models: Pointer meter with adjustable contacts, optical vane or inductive pickups
entries.measured_or_indicated: Analog value plus one or more control thresholds
quirks_to_simulate: Set pointers are manually dragged around the scale
contact pressure can momentarily pin the indicating hand
relay chatter and separate reset point
optical variants avoid drag but can mis-trigger on dust

sources.title: WIKA switch contacts in measuring instruments

image_links.title: Wikimedia Commons images: analog contact meter relay adjustable setpoint

entries.name: Flip-dot / flip-disc display
entries.representative_names_models: Ferranti-Packard and Luminator transit signs; AlfaZeta modules
entries.measured_or_indicated: Text, numerals, symbols, bar graphs or mimic diagrams
quirks_to_simulate: Electromagnetic discs physically flip between two colours
bistable image with no holding power

image_links.title: Wikimedia Commons images: flip dot display close up

entries.name: Split-flap display
entries.representative_names_models: Solari Cifra 5; Solari Udine airport boards; Gino Valle designs
entries.measured_or_indicated: Text, time, destinations, prices or status
quirks_to_simulate: Characters appear only after cycling through intervening flaps
one module can lag the rest
power loss freezes the last state

image_links.title: Wikimedia Commons images: Solari split flap display airport board

entries.name: Edgewise meter
entries.representative_names_models: Weston and GE edgewise switchboard meters; rack-mounted process indicators
entries.measured_or_indicated: Electrical or process variables in dense panels
quirks_to_simulate: pointer moves like a vertical blade
reduced panel height but increased parallax
neighbouring meters form a trend display
damping and hard stops remain conventional moving-coil mechanics

image_links.title: Wikimedia Commons images: vintage edgewise panel meter

entries.name: Ribbon and tape meter
entries.representative_names_models: Moving-colour ribbon indicators; aircraft vertical tape instruments; process tape meters
entries.measured_or_indicated: Speed, altitude, temperature, level or arbitrary trend variable
quirks_to_simulate: A coloured band or printed tape moves behind a narrow window
large apparent travel in little panel width
digits can be half hidden at the window edge

image_links.title: Wikimedia Commons images: vintage ribbon tape meter instrument

entries.name: Roller, drum and cyclometer indicator
entries.representative_names_models: Rolling-number frequency meters; drum speedometers; counter drums paired with a pointer
entries.measured_or_indicated: Discrete or quasi-continuous numeric value
quirks_to_simulate: two numerals may be partly visible
a decimal drum can move continuously while higher drums snap
rollover from 9 to 0 is visually distinctive

image_links.title: Wikimedia Commons images: rolling drum numeric indicator gauge

entries.name: Concentric multi-pointer instrument
entries.representative_names_models: Dual/triple aircraft engine gauges; clock-like multiple shafts; duplex process meters
entries.measured_or_indicated: Several related variables in one aperture
quirks_to_simulate: Pointers cross, overlap and cast separate shadows
one movement may obscure another’s hub
failures can leave one hand frozen while others continue

image_links.title: Wikimedia Commons images: aircraft concentric multi pointer engine gauge

entries.name: Barber-pole warning indicator
entries.representative_names_models: Striped rotating drum or sliding flag for overspeed, unsafe gear, cabin altitude and control limits
entries.measured_or_indicated: Binary warning or moving limit boundary
quirks_to_simulate: Diagonal stripes create a conspicuous moving texture
drum may rotate into view or a striped sector may track a limit
snap action, chatter near threshold and partial exposure
animation should not resemble a smooth digital fill bar

image_links.title: Wikimedia Commons images: aircraft barber pole warning indicator

entries.name: Incandescent pilot lamp and annunciator
entries.representative_names_models: Miniature bayonet bulbs; aircraft post lights; jewel lamps; warning panels
entries.measured_or_indicated: Binary status, alarm, backlight or legend illumination
quirks_to_simulate: brightness and colour temperature vary strongly with voltage
shared supply droops when many lamps light
filament sag, blackened bulbs and random failures
warm lenses bloom into nearby legends

image_links.title: Wikimedia Commons images: vintage incandescent panel indicator jewel lamp

entries.name: Edge-lit engraved acrylic and light-pipe display
entries.representative_names_models: Aircraft edge-lit panels; engraved Perspex legends; automotive light pipes
entries.measured_or_indicated: Fixed legends, scales, pointers and annunciators
quirks_to_simulate: lamp position creates bright and dim regions
failed bulbs create asymmetric pools rather than uniform darkness

image_links.title: Wikimedia Commons images: edge lit acrylic aircraft instrument panel

entries.name: Shadow meter / moving-vane tuning indicator
entries.representative_names_models: Philips shadow meter; pre-war radio tuning indicators
entries.measured_or_indicated: Radio tuning strength, balance or null
quirks_to_simulate: A lamp projects the shadow of a moving vane
shadow width or position is the reading
room light can wash it out
response is often nonlinear near the null

image_links.title: Wikimedia Commons images: vintage radio shadow meter tuning indicator

entries.name: Electron-ray “magic eye” tube
entries.representative_names_models: 6E5, EM34, EM84 and similar tuning indicators
entries.measured_or_indicated: Radio tuning, signal level, recording level or balance
quirks_to_simulate: Green phosphor wedge or bar opens/closes rather than moving like a pointer
grid overdrive can make sectors overlap
room light strongly affects apparent contrast

image_links.title: Wikimedia Commons images: 6E5 EM34 magic eye tube glowing

entries.name: Nixie numeric cold-cathode tube
entries.representative_names_models: Burroughs NIXIE; IN-12, IN-14, ZM1040 and NL-5441
entries.measured_or_indicated: Numerals, decimal points and occasional symbols
quirks_to_simulate: Each digit is a separate wire cathode at a different depth and shape
cathode poisoning on rarely used digits

image_links.title: Wikimedia Commons images: Nixie tube digits close up

entries.name: Alphanumeric Nixie tube
entries.representative_names_models: Burroughs B-7971 and B-8971; segmented or shaped-cathode alphabet tubes
entries.measured_or_indicated: Letters, numerals and symbols
quirks_to_simulate: Characters are assembled from unusual long cathode segments or individual shapes
slow or ghosted character changes under multiplex drive

sources.title: Computer History Museum: Burroughs B-7971 alphanumeric tube

image_links.title: Wikimedia Commons images: Burroughs B-7971 alphanumeric Nixie

entries.name: Pixie / top-view cold-cathode display
entries.representative_names_models: Philips ZM1050 and ZM1051; Burroughs Pixie variants
entries.measured_or_indicated: Numerals and symbols
quirks_to_simulate: Digits glow through shaped apertures in a top electrode rather than as free wire numerals
aperture position and obscuration differ by digit
high-voltage ignition and ageing remain gas-discharge behaviours

sources.title: Computer History Museum: Pixie/ZM1050 display tube

image_links.title: Wikimedia Commons images: Philips ZM1050 Pixie display tube

entries.name: Panaplex planar gas-discharge display
entries.representative_names_models: Burroughs Panaplex II; Sperry planar numeric panels
entries.measured_or_indicated: Multi-digit numeric and segmented readouts
quirks_to_simulate: Flat orange neon segments share a glass panel
less depth than Nixie but still visibly gaseous

image_links.title: Wikimedia Commons images: Burroughs Panaplex display

entries.name: Dekatron / glow-transfer counting tube
entries.representative_names_models: Ericsson and Mullard GC10-series; ten-position and divide-by-ten tubes
entries.measured_or_indicated: Counting state, divider position or rotating indicator
quirks_to_simulate: A single glow dot walks around a ring of cathodes
guide electrodes can cause two-stage motion
missed counts, reverse stepping and stalls

sources.title: Computer History Museum: Dekatron counting tube

image_links.title: Wikimedia Commons images: Dekatron counting tube glowing dot

entries.name: Linear neon glow bar
entries.representative_names_models: Soviet IN-9 and IN-13; Burroughs linear indicators
entries.measured_or_indicated: Analog level, tuning, audio level or process value
quirks_to_simulate: Glow length follows current but is not perfectly linear
orange column can detach or extinguish at low current

image_links.title: Wikimedia Commons images: IN-13 IN-9 neon bargraph tube

entries.name: Numitron / Minitron incandescent segment display
entries.representative_names_models: RCA Numitron; IEE and Apollo-era Minitron; DR2000/DR2110
entries.measured_or_indicated: Numeric or limited alphanumeric display
quirks_to_simulate: Tiny tungsten filaments glow warm yellow-white
brightness depends on supply and filament age
more lit segments can visibly load a weak/shared supply
decimal points often differ in colour and lag

image_links.title: Wikimedia Commons images: Numitron Minitron incandescent display

entries.name: Vacuum fluorescent display
entries.representative_names_models: Noritake Itron; Futaba and NEC calculator, audio and automotive modules
entries.measured_or_indicated: Numeric, alphanumeric, bargraph and custom icons
quirks_to_simulate: Blue-green phosphor with visible filament wires
high viewing angle and characteristic diffuse bloom through tinted glass

sources.title: Noritake Itron: VFD operation and design effects

image_links.title: Wikimedia Commons images: vacuum fluorescent display VFD close up filament

entries.name: LED seven-segment display
entries.representative_names_models: Monsanto/HP early modules; HP 5082 series; Kingbright and Lite-On modules
entries.measured_or_indicated: Numeric readout
quirks_to_simulate: count-dependent dimming occurs with a weak supply, shared resistor or shared current budget—not with a correctly regulated constant-current design

sources.title: Texas Instruments: LED-display brightness uniformity and ghosting
Analog Devices: MAX7219 multiplexed LED driver

image_links.title: Wikimedia Commons images: vintage red LED seven segment display

entries.name: LED 14- and 16-segment starburst display
entries.representative_names_models: DL-1414 smart display; HDSP alphanumeric modules; avionics and test gear
entries.measured_or_indicated: Letters, numerals and symbols
quirks_to_simulate: shared current limits can dim dense letters
failed segment changes one glyph into another rather than merely losing a bar

image_links.title: Wikimedia Commons images: vintage LED 16 segment alphanumeric display

entries.name: LED bargraph and moving-dot display
entries.representative_names_models: LM3914/LM3915 meters; automotive bar gauges; audio peak bars
entries.measured_or_indicated: Level, percentage, frequency bands or threshold state
quirks_to_simulate: discrete thresholds chatter with noisy input
a peak dot may decay separately
current budget makes full bars dimmer in poor designs

image_links.title: Wikimedia Commons images: LED bargraph LM3914 display

entries.name: LED dot-matrix and smart alphanumeric module
entries.representative_names_models: 5x7 matrices; HP intelligent displays; scrolling message modules
entries.measured_or_indicated: Text, symbols, plots and coarse gauges
quirks_to_simulate: brightness varies with number of active dots unless current is compensated
power-up test patterns, random garbage or a brief blank are period-authentic

sources.title: Texas Instruments: LED-display brightness uniformity and ghosting

image_links.title: Wikimedia Commons images: vintage LED dot matrix smart display

entries.name: Monochrome storage plasma panel
entries.representative_names_models: University of Illinois PLATO panels; Owens-Illinois and IBM orange plasma displays
entries.measured_or_indicated: Text, graphics, terminals and instrument pages
quirks_to_simulate: Each orange cell behaves like a tiny neon lamp and can be electrically bistable
persistence is electrical memory, not merely phosphor decay

sources.title: University of Illinois: Plasma display invention

image_links.title: Wikimedia Commons images: orange monochrome plasma display PLATO

entries.name: Powder and thin-film electroluminescent display
entries.representative_names_models: EL panels; Lumineq/Beneq rugged displays; automotive backlights
entries.measured_or_indicated: Fixed legends, segmented readouts or monochrome matrix graphics
quirks_to_simulate: brightness falls with age and drive
very wide temperature operation and viewing angle

image_links.title: Wikimedia Commons images: electroluminescent instrument display panel

entries.name: Dynamic-scattering-mode LCD
entries.representative_names_models: RCA experimental and early watch/calculator displays
entries.measured_or_indicated: Numeric and simple segmented readout
quirks_to_simulate: Energised segments become milky/scattering rather than dark
early examples often look silvery-white rather than the later black-on-grey LCD stereotype

sources.title: IEEE Spectrum: RCA and the first liquid-crystal displays

image_links.title: Wikimedia Commons images: dynamic scattering LCD early RCA display

entries.name: Twisted-nematic segmented LCD
entries.representative_names_models: Watches, calculators, meters and custom industrial glass
entries.measured_or_indicated: Numeric, icons, bars and fixed legends
quirks_to_simulate: Strong viewing-angle dependence and contrast inversion
temperature-dependent response, especially sluggish in cold
reflective, transmissive and transflective modes look different
weak elastomer contacts cause missing rows

sources.title: IEEE Spectrum: RCA and the first liquid-crystal displays

image_links.title: Wikimedia Commons images: segmented twisted nematic LCD meter

entries.name: STN/FSTN passive-matrix LCD
entries.representative_names_models: Industrial HMIs, telephones, test instruments and vehicle displays
entries.measured_or_indicated: Text, graphics and segmented or dot-matrix gauges
quirks_to_simulate: FSTN film improves neutrality but adds its own polarisation tint

image_links.title: Wikimedia Commons images: STN FSTN monochrome LCD instrument

entries.name: Cholesteric and other bistable LCD
entries.representative_names_models: Kent Displays writing panels; reflective shelf labels and status indicators
entries.measured_or_indicated: Persistent text, symbols or status with near-zero holding power
quirks_to_simulate: pressure marks can temporarily disturb the texture

image_links.title: Wikimedia Commons images: cholesteric bistable LCD display

entries.name: Segmented electrophoretic / e-paper display
entries.representative_names_models: E Ink segmented labels, meters, thermostats and low-power indicators
entries.measured_or_indicated: Persistent numbers, icons, bars and labels
quirks_to_simulate: No power needed to hold an image
excellent diffuse-light readability but no inherent glow
physical segments can have rounded, slightly mottled fill

sources.title: E Ink: Electronic-paper history and technology

image_links.title: Wikimedia Commons images: segmented e ink e paper display gauge

entries.name: Electrochromic segmented display
entries.representative_names_models: Viologen, tungsten-oxide and automotive mirror-derived indicators
entries.measured_or_indicated: Persistent or semi-persistent symbols, numbers and tint levels
quirks_to_simulate: Colour front grows and bleaches over time
switching is much slower than LED
limited cycle life and permanent staining
temperature strongly affects speed and depth of colour

image_links.title: Wikimedia Commons images: electrochromic segmented display

entries.name: Segmented OLED and passive-matrix OLED
entries.representative_names_models: Small instrument modules; automotive tell-tales; PMOLED text displays
entries.measured_or_indicated: Numbers, icons, text and bar graphs
quirks_to_simulate: True black and wide viewing angle
extremely fast pixels but driver may use PWM
bright static segments dim over years

image_links.title: Wikimedia Commons images: segmented OLED instrument display

entries.name: CRT oscilloscope, vector and radar display
entries.representative_names_models: Round radar PPI; rectangular oscilloscope; vector monitors and engine analysers
entries.measured_or_indicated: Waveforms, range/bearing, vectors or computed traces

image_links.title: Wikimedia Commons images: vintage radar PPI CRT oscilloscope display

entries.name: Storage CRT
entries.representative_names_models: Tektronix direct-view bistable storage tubes; radar and analytical instruments
entries.measured_or_indicated: Waveforms or traces that must remain without refresh
quirks_to_simulate: erase cycle flashes or floods the screen
stored trace blooms and spreads with repeated writing

image_links.title: Wikimedia Commons images: Tektronix storage CRT oscilloscope

entries.name: Head-up and collimated projected symbology
entries.representative_names_models: Reflector gunsights; aircraft HUDs; automotive combiner HUDs
entries.measured_or_indicated: Speed, attitude, aiming, navigation and warnings at optical infinity
quirks_to_simulate: collimation means symbols appear distant while dust stays on the glass
head movement clips or shifts imagery
focus and parallax errors are installation-specific

image_links.title: Wikimedia Commons images: vintage aircraft head up display HUD symbology

entries.name: Electrowetting and interferometric MEMS display
entries.representative_names_models: Liquavista electrowetting; Qualcomm Mirasol interferometric modulator
entries.measured_or_indicated: Reflective low-power text, colour graphics or segmented indicators
quirks_to_simulate: Very fast reflective switching but angle-dependent colour
oil-film breakup, pinning and stuck pixels
suitable as an exotic boundary before generic raster screens

image_links.title: Wikimedia Commons images: electrowetting MEMS display pixel

entries.name: Irreversible chemical and time-temperature indicator
entries.representative_names_models: Time-temperature integrators; freeze indicators; sterilisation tape; gas exposure badges
entries.measured_or_indicated: Cumulative thermal, chemical or environmental exposure
quirks_to_simulate: diffusion front rather than a crisp electronic change
ideal for witness-state modelling where history matters more than current input

image_links.title: Wikimedia Commons images: time temperature indicator chemical exposure label

entries.name: Three-pointer altimeter
entries.representative_names_models: Classic sensitive altimeter with 100-, 1,000- and 10,000-foot hands
entries.measured_or_indicated: Pressure altitude
entries.era: c.1920s-c.1970s common; some remain in service
quirks_to_simulate: a small 10,000-foot pointer is easy to miss
NASA documented serious misreading risk, especially near pointer overlaps

sources.title: NASA: Altimeter display types and reading performance
FAA: Pilot’s Handbook of Aeronautical Knowledge, Flight Instruments

image_links.title: Wikimedia Commons images: three pointer aircraft altimeter

entries.name: Counter-pointer altimeter
entries.representative_names_models: Drum counter for thousands plus one fine pointer for hundreds
entries.measured_or_indicated: Pressure altitude
entries.era: c.1940s-present; common in transport and military aircraft mid-century
quirks_to_simulate: Pointer supplies fine altitude while digits supply coarse place value
counter carry can occur while pointer crosses zero
vibration can make the counter shimmer

sources.title: NASA: Altimeter display types and reading performance
FAA: Pilot’s Handbook of Aeronautical Knowledge, Flight Instruments

image_links.title: Wikimedia Commons images: counter pointer aircraft altimeter

entries.name: Drum-pointer and counter-drum altimeter
entries.representative_names_models: Kollsman-style drum-pointer; counter-drum-pointer displays studied by NASA
entries.measured_or_indicated: Pressure altitude
quirks_to_simulate: Rolling drums reduce hand-counting but introduce transition ambiguity
drum can be masked until valid
pointer often makes one revolution per 1,000 feet
carry is intentionally delayed or accelerated
baro setting and low-altitude warning sectors

sources.title: NASA: Altimeter display types and reading performance

image_links.title: Wikimedia Commons images: counter drum pointer altimeter

entries.name: Airspeed indicator with coloured operating arcs
entries.representative_names_models: Pitot-static ASI; white, green, yellow arcs and red radial line
entries.measured_or_indicated: Indicated airspeed derived from dynamic pressure
quirks_to_simulate: Nonlinear square-root relationship between pressure and speed
overspeed can drive into a peg

sources.title: FAA: Pilot’s Handbook of Aeronautical Knowledge, Flight Instruments

image_links.title: Wikimedia Commons images: aircraft airspeed indicator colored arcs

entries.name: Vertical-speed indicator
entries.representative_names_models: Calibrated-leak diaphragm VSI; instantaneous VSI variants
entries.measured_or_indicated: Rate of climb or descent
quirks_to_simulate: Conventional units deliberately lag by several seconds
scale may be linear near zero and compressed at extremes

sources.title: FAA: Pilot’s Handbook of Aeronautical Knowledge, Flight Instruments

image_links.title: Wikimedia Commons images: aircraft vertical speed indicator VSI

entries.name: Mechanical attitude indicator / artificial horizon
entries.representative_names_models: Vacuum- or electrically driven gyro horizon; caged aerobatic variants
entries.measured_or_indicated: Aircraft pitch and bank attitude
quirks_to_simulate: bank scale and sky/ground drum move in opposite visual ways across designs
warning flag and lazy settling after uncage

sources.title: FAA: Pilot’s Handbook of Aeronautical Knowledge, Flight Instruments

image_links.title: Wikimedia Commons images: vintage aircraft attitude indicator artificial horizon

entries.name: Turn-and-bank, turn coordinator and slip-skid ball
entries.representative_names_models: Needle-and-ball instrument; miniature-aircraft turn coordinator
entries.measured_or_indicated: Turn rate and coordination/lateral acceleration
quirks_to_simulate: Gyro needle has its own lag and damping
ball sloshes in a curved liquid tube and may stick
turn coordinator senses roll as well as yaw
fluid bubble size and tube curvature matter

sources.title: FAA: Pilot’s Handbook of Aeronautical Knowledge, Flight Instruments

image_links.title: Wikimedia Commons images: turn and bank indicator slip skid ball

entries.name: Radar altimeter dial
entries.representative_names_models: Low-range radio/radar altimeter with decision-height bug and warning flag
entries.measured_or_indicated: Height above terrain
quirks_to_simulate: Needle is meaningful only at low altitude
terrain and bank cause lively movement
scale often expands near the ground and compresses high values

image_links.title: Wikimedia Commons images: vintage radar altimeter dial decision height bug

entries.name: RMI and HSI multi-pointer navigation indicator
entries.representative_names_models: Bendix radio magnetic indicator; horizontal situation indicator
entries.measured_or_indicated: Magnetic heading, selected course, bearing and deviation
quirks_to_simulate: Rotating compass card plus one or two bearing needles
pointers cross and can hide flags

image_links.title: Wikimedia Commons images: vintage RMI HSI aircraft instrument

entries.name: Aircraft dual- and triple-pointer engine gauge
entries.representative_names_models: Manifold pressure/RPM; EGT/fuel flow; torque/temperature; triple tachometer
entries.measured_or_indicated: Two or three engine or propeller variables
quirks_to_simulate: one failed movement may freeze while the others work
strong hub parallax and shadow stacking

sources.title: FAA: Pilot’s Handbook of Aeronautical Knowledge, Flight Instruments

image_links.title: Wikimedia Commons images: aircraft dual triple pointer engine gauge

entries.name: Railway duplex brake and vacuum gauge
entries.representative_names_models: Budenberg and Westinghouse duplex gauges; red and black needles
entries.measured_or_indicated: Brake-pipe, reservoir, cylinder or vacuum pressures
entries.era: 1870s-present on heritage and some service stock
quirks_to_simulate: Two pressure systems share one face
red/black hands and labels vary by railway
brake applications produce pulses and delayed equalisation
train-pipe leakage causes a slow creep
one needle can mask the other

image_links.title: Wikimedia Commons images: railway duplex brake vacuum pressure gauge

entries.name: Steam-boiler water gauge glass
entries.representative_names_models: Tubular gauge glass; reflex water gauge; tri-cock checks
entries.measured_or_indicated: Boiler water level
quirks_to_simulate: swell and shrink with steam demand
glass can appear full when passages are blocked

image_links.title: Wikimedia Commons images: steam boiler water gauge glass reflex

entries.name: Engine-order telegraph / Chadburn
entries.representative_names_models: Bridge and engine-room paired dials; Chadburns and pedestal telegraphs
entries.measured_or_indicated: Commanded engine direction/speed and acknowledgement
quirks_to_simulate: Bridge handle moves command pointer and rings a bell
engine room repeats the handle to acknowledge
polished brass, engraved sectors and mechanical clatter are intrinsic

sources.title: Herreshoff Marine Museum: Engine-room signal gear

image_links.title: Wikimedia Commons images: antique Chadburn engine order telegraph

entries.name: Rotating-neon fathometer
entries.representative_names_models: Dorsey Fathometer; Submarine Signal Company Model 808
entries.measured_or_indicated: Water depth from acoustic echo time
quirks_to_simulate: A neon flash seen through a rotating dial appears stationary at the echo depth
multiple echoes and bottom hardness create extra flashes

sources.title: Engineering and Technology History Wiki: Fathometer

image_links.title: Wikimedia Commons images: Dorsey rotating neon fathometer indicator

entries.name: Mechanical-wheel flasher sonar
entries.representative_names_models: Vexilar FL/FLX series and earlier ice-fishing flashers
entries.measured_or_indicated: Current sonar return versus depth
quirks_to_simulate: A high-speed wheel carries fixed lights that flash at timed positions
bottom lock and zoom remap the scale

sources.title: Vexilar FLX-28 manual: how flashers work

image_links.title: Wikimedia Commons images: Vexilar mechanical flasher sonar display

entries.name: VU meter / standard volume indicator
entries.representative_names_models: Bell Labs/CBS/NBC VU standard; Sifam and Weston audio meters
entries.measured_or_indicated: Perceived programme level, not instantaneous peak
quirks_to_simulate: Specified mechanical ballistics deliberately average audio and miss short peaks
logarithmic dB scale with 0 VU near the right
pointer trembles with bass and speech rhythm

image_links.title: Wikimedia Commons images: vintage VU meter Sifam Weston

entries.name: Peak programme meter
entries.representative_names_models: BBC Type I; European PPMs with numbered or dB scales
entries.measured_or_indicated: Quasi-peak audio programme level
quirks_to_simulate: Very fast attack and deliberately slow, nearly constant-rate fall
short peaks are integrated rather than shown literally
overload lamps and peak hold may coexist

sources.title: BBC R&D Report 1938-13: Development of a peak programme meter

image_links.title: Wikimedia Commons images: BBC peak programme meter PPM

entries.name: Radio S-meter
entries.representative_names_models: Receiver signal-strength meter, usually S1-S9 then dB above S9
entries.measured_or_indicated: Relative received radio signal strength
quirks_to_simulate: Driven by AGC rather than a precision detector
low end is often compressed and receiver-specific
S9 conventionally corresponds to 50 microvolts on HF but real sets vary
needle rises fast and falls with AGC decay

sources.title: ARRL: Practical S-meter calibration and nonlinearity

image_links.title: Wikimedia Commons images: vintage radio S meter analog

entries.name: Geiger-counter analog ratemeter
entries.representative_names_models: Victoreen, Ludlum and civil-defence survey meters with GM tube
entries.measured_or_indicated: Radiation count rate or inferred dose rate
quirks_to_simulate: Individual random pulses create authentic Poisson twitch and clicks
RC averaging makes fast rise/slow settling
detector dead time causes high-rate nonlinearity or saturation

sources.title: US NRC: Geiger-Mueller counter test procedure

image_links.title: Wikimedia Commons images: vintage Geiger counter analog meter

entries.name: Ribbon, rolling-drum and “thermometer” automotive speedometer
entries.representative_names_models: 1950s-1970s American ribbon speedometers; Citroën rotating drum; linear colour band displays
entries.measured_or_indicated: Road speed and sometimes odometer
quirks_to_simulate: Colour ribbon length or drum position replaces a conventional hand
digits can roll behind a mask

image_links.title: Wikimedia Commons images: vintage ribbon speedometer rolling drum dashboard

entries.name: Automotive econometer / manifold-vacuum gauge
entries.representative_names_models: Vacuum economy gauges in 1950s-1980s cars; aftermarket “mileage” meters
entries.measured_or_indicated: Engine manifold vacuum as a rough load/economy proxy
quirks_to_simulate: High vacuum usually reads economical and low vacuum reads power, so scale meaning is inverse to throttle
coloured ECON/POWER sectors overstate precision—it is not a true fuel-flow measurement

image_links.title: Wikimedia Commons images: vintage automotive econometer vacuum gauge

entries.name: Storm glass / FitzRoy weather glass
entries.representative_names_models: Camphor, salts, alcohol and water in a sealed glass
entries.measured_or_indicated: Claimed weather forecast; actually mainly temperature-driven crystallisation
quirks_to_simulate: Crystals grow, dissolve and form threads or flakes over hours
patterns are beautiful but scientifically weak
enclosure temperature and prior history dominate. A splendid liar in a glass bottle

sources.title: History of Science Museum Oxford: Storm glass

image_links.title: Wikimedia Commons images: FitzRoy storm glass crystals

entries.name: Railway block, semaphore and route repeater
entries.representative_names_models: Miniature signal-arm repeaters; block telegraph needles; route-indicator shutters
entries.measured_or_indicated: Track occupancy, signal state, route or permission
quirks_to_simulate: snap between named states rather than interpolate
needles can rest at LINE CLEAR, TRAIN ON LINE or NORMAL

image_links.title: Wikimedia Commons images: railway block telegraph semaphore repeater instrument

entries.name: Mechanical/electromechanical vertical-tape flight instrument
entries.representative_names_models: Vertical-scale airspeed, altitude and engine instruments; moving printed belts
entries.measured_or_indicated: Flight or engine variable with a moving linear scale
quirks_to_simulate: The scale moves while a fixed index stays put
belt wrap, speed limit and direction can confuse
printed numbers enter and leave a narrow window
NASA found space savings did not automatically improve readability

image_links.title: Wikimedia Commons images: vintage vertical tape aircraft instrument

entries.name: Follow-the-pointer photographic exposure meter
entries.representative_names_models: Weston Master; Gossen and Sekonic selenium/CdS meters with match-needle rings
entries.measured_or_indicated: Scene luminance, exposure value, aperture/shutter combinations
quirks_to_simulate: Meter needle is matched by rotating a second pointer or circle
logarithmic EV and coupled calculator scales
selenium cells age and respond slowly in dim light
no batteries in many selenium models

image_links.title: Wikimedia Commons images: Weston Master selenium exposure meter analog

entries.name: Goethe weather glass / water barometer
entries.representative_names_models: Sealed bulb with open spout and coloured water
entries.measured_or_indicated: Atmospheric pressure, confounded by temperature
quirks_to_simulate: Liquid rises in the spout as external pressure falls
trapped-air temperature creates large false movement
scale is often qualitative “storm/change/fair” rather than calibrated

image_links.title: Wikimedia Commons images: Goethe water barometer weather glass

entries.name: Tempest Prognosticator / leech barometer
entries.representative_names_models: George Merryweather’s 1851 twelve-bottle leech instrument
entries.measured_or_indicated: Claimed storm warning from animal behaviour
quirks_to_simulate: Twelve leeches climb tubes and trip whalebone linkages to ring a bell
events are discrete, delayed and biologically inconsistent
maintenance includes living sensors, which is a sentence modern product managers should perhaps not hear

image_links.title: Wikimedia Commons images: Tempest Prognosticator leech barometer

entries.name: Mechanical moving-map and roller-map display
entries.representative_names_models: Aircraft dead-reckoning moving maps; scrolling road-map navigators; roller chart displays
entries.measured_or_indicated: Estimated position, route progress or terrain beneath vehicle
quirks_to_simulate: Printed map physically scrolls under a fixed aircraft/vehicle symbol
route continues moving even when the estimate is wrong

image_links.title: Wikimedia Commons images: vintage mechanical aircraft moving map roller display

entries.name: Automotive tell-tale / “idiot light” cluster
entries.representative_names_models: Oil-pressure, charge, coolant and brake warning lamps; bulb-check circuits
entries.measured_or_indicated: Binary fault or threshold state
quirks_to_simulate: alternator lamp glows dimly under small voltage difference
shared grounds make unrelated lamps ghost
failed bulb silently removes the warning

image_links.title: Wikimedia Commons images: vintage automotive warning light telltale cluster

entries.name: Mechanical float tide gauge and stilling-well recorder
entries.representative_names_models: Standard tide gauge with float, pulley, clock and pen drum; tide staff
entries.measured_or_indicated: Sea level versus time
entries.era: c.1830s-c.1980s dominant; historical and some backup use
quirks_to_simulate: Stilling well filters waves and adds phase lag
storm surge can run off scale
remote pointer stations sometimes showed harbour level in real time

sources.title: NOAA: changing technology for tide measurements

image_links.title: Wikimedia Commons images: mechanical float tide gauge chart recorder

entries.name: Mechanical seismograph and smoked-paper drum
entries.representative_names_models: Wiechert, Milne and Wood-Anderson instruments; helicorders
entries.measured_or_indicated: Ground displacement, velocity or acceleration over time
quirks_to_simulate: Suspended mass remains inertial while frame moves
pen scratches smoked paper or light exposes photographic paper
trace saturates or runs off drum in large events

image_links.title: Wikimedia Commons images: antique mechanical seismograph smoked paper drum

entries.name: Railway speed indicator and recorder
entries.representative_names_models: Hasler/Teloc and Hasler-Hausshalter instruments; Flaman recorders
entries.measured_or_indicated: Locomotive speed plus a tamper-resistant time history
quirks_to_simulate: Large speed needle plus sealed chart or paper tape
pointer and recorder may disagree slightly

sources.title: Science Museum: Hasler speed indicator and recorder

image_links.title: Wikimedia Commons images: Hasler Teloc locomotive speed recorder

entries.name: Synchroscope
entries.representative_names_models: Weston and switchboard synchronising instruments with FAST/SLOW arrows
entries.measured_or_indicated: Frequency difference and phase angle between AC sources
quirks_to_simulate: Pointer rotates continuously when frequencies differ, with direction showing fast or slow
operator closes breaker near the twelve-o’clock mark
jitter and reversals near synchronism are normal

image_links.title: Wikimedia Commons images: vintage analog synchroscope fast slow pointer

entries.name: Gold-leaf or aluminium-leaf electroscope
entries.representative_names_models: Bennet gold-leaf electroscope; Braun electroscope
entries.measured_or_indicated: Presence, sign or rough magnitude of electric charge
quirks_to_simulate: Leaves diverge with charge and collapse through leakage
humidity and dirty insulation accelerate decay
response is nonlinear and easily saturated
charge can remain after the source is removed
no honest precision scale in most versions

image_links.title: Wikimedia Commons images: gold leaf electroscope instrument

entries.name: Tachograph / recording speedometer
entries.representative_names_models: Kienzle and Veeder-Root paper-disc tachographs
entries.measured_or_indicated: Vehicle speed, distance, time and driver activity history
quirks_to_simulate: speed needle and recorded line may lag differently

image_links.title: Wikimedia Commons images: vintage paper disc tachograph speedometer

entries.name: Mechanical G-meter with min/max witness needles
entries.representative_names_models: Aircraft accelerometer with central live pointer and resettable positive/negative peak hands
entries.measured_or_indicated: Normal acceleration / load factor
quirks_to_simulate: Centre at 1 g rather than zero
two drag needles remain at extrema
abrupt manoeuvres cause overshoot and peg bounce
vibration leaves a small false peak

image_links.title: Wikimedia Commons images: vintage aircraft G meter accelerometer witness pointers
